headline: Airline Stocks Soar on Falling Oil Prices and Delta’s Rosy Forecast
summary: Shares of U.S. airlines are climbing after Delta Air Lines said customers haven’t been fazed by higher flight prices and the cease-fire with Iran sent oil prices tumbling.  Delta Chief Executive Ed Bastian said strong demand for air travel hasn’t taken a hit since airlines started hiking up fares and fees to offset higher jet fuel costs.  The biggest concern for the year ahead will be long jet-fuel prices remain elevated, Bastian said.

raw.headline: Airline Stocks Soar on Falling Oil Prices and Delta’s Rosy Forecast
raw.summary: Shares of U.S. airlines are climbing after Delta Air Lines said customers haven’t been fazed by higher flight prices and the cease-fire with Iran sent oil prices tumbling.  Delta Chief Executive Ed Bastian said strong demand for air travel hasn’t taken a hit since airlines started hiking up fares and fees to offset higher jet fuel costs.  The biggest concern for the year ahead will be long jet-fuel prices remain elevated, Bastian said.

headline: Oil Prices Bounce; Iran Says Hormuz Opening Hinges On Israel, But S&P 500 Rallies
summary: Crude oil prices plunged more than $20 a barrel from Tuesday's peak after President Donald Trump agreed to a two-week ceasefire and Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  United Airlines, Carnival and fellow cruise and airline stocks still ranked among top S&P 500 winners in morning trade.  Gold, silver and copper prices all jumped, fueling gains for Newmont and Freeport-McMoRan, though they came well off their highs.

Gold, silver and copper prices all jumped, fueling gains for Newmont and Freeport-McMoRan, though they came well off their highs.

headline: Delta Air Lines Stock Surges After Earnings. How It’s Shrugging Off $2 Billion Fuel Hit.
summary: Delta Air Lines  stock was surging ahead of the open Wednesday as the carrier’s earnings and the two-week U.S., Iran cease-fire deal proved to be tailwinds for the shares.  Demand is so strong that Delta expects to post a $1 billion pretax profit in the current quarter despite a more than $2 billion increase in fuel costs.  The carrier is also cutting capacity to mitigate the impact of higher fuel prices, it said.

headline: Shares of airline companies are trading higher in response to President Trump's social media post indicating that the U.S will work closely with Iran and that many of the 15 points in the proposed plan to end the war have been agreed to.
